title: New insights into ‘les interminables listes nominatives d’esclaves’ from numerical analyses of the personnel in Angkorian inscriptions - Persée
highlights: A great number of people were employed to maintain the Angkor kingdom’s much-studied stone temples — many living and working in their shadow, providing ritual food, clothing, music and dance for tens of thousands of stone deities housed within. For example, inscription K. 908 (CE 1191) records that people in 8,176 villages provided rice for 20,400 gods in gold, silver, bronze and stone in the provinces. highlights: Between the 9th and 15th centuries CE the builders and artisans of Angkor and its territories furnished their landscapes with thousands of temples and tens of thousands of sacred sculptures, yet the operational behaviour of these craft specialists is little known.
